coreCompetencies: Basic understanding of the software lifecycle and where testing fits
Elementary knowledge of test case design and test scenarios
Ability to execute manual test scripts and record results
Basic understanding of defect reporting and severity levels
Familiarity with a defect tracking tool for logging issues
Elementary understanding of test environments (dev, staging, production)
Ability to run guided exploratory sessions on simple features
complementaryCompetencies: Basic familiarity with web browsers and their developer tools
Familiarity with agile ceremonies and their purpose
Elementary understanding of databases for checking data
indicators: Requires close supervision on testing tasks
Executes documented test cases with guidance
Learning to distinguish severity from priority

coreCompetencies: Ability to write clear, detailed test cases from acceptance criteria
Execution of full functional test passes across a feature
Practical understanding of regression testing and when to run it
Ability to reproduce, isolate and document defects precisely
Familiarity with test suites maintained by automation engineers
Ability to validate data using simple SQL queries
Participation in refinement to spot untestable requirements
complementaryCompetencies: Basic understanding of cross-browser and cross-device checks
Familiarity with usability heuristics
Elementary understanding of accessibility checks
indicators: Plans and runs tests for a feature with moderate supervision
Reports defects developers can act on without back-and-forth
Collaborates with developers to confirm fixes

coreCompetencies: Design of risk-based test plans for a feature or component
Structured exploratory and session-based testing with charters
Ability to run root cause analysis on recurring defects
Definition of entry and exit criteria for test cycles
Coordination of defect triage with developers and product
Ability to interpret results from automated suites owned by the automation team
Estimation of testing effort for planned work
complementaryCompetencies: Familiarity with test management tools and traceability
Understanding of compatibility and localization checks
Elementary understanding of accessibility standards (WCAG)
indicators: Owns the test approach for a feature end to end
Surfaces quality risks proactively during planning
Contributes improvements to the team's QA process

coreCompetencies: Definition of the test strategy for a product or major workstream
Prioritization of testing by risk, impact and change frequency
Facilitation of shift-left practices so defects surface earlier
Coordination of user acceptance testing with business stakeholders
Design of a defect management workflow with clear severities and SLAs
Definition of quality metrics such as escape rate and coverage of critical paths
Guidance of exploratory testing across a team
complementaryCompetencies: Understanding of performance and security test needs to brief specialists
Familiarity with test data management for realistic scenarios
Understanding of accessibility acceptance criteria
indicators: Leads the quality approach for a medium-sized product
Sets testing standards the team follows
Mentors junior QA on test design and defect analysis

coreCompetencies: Ownership of the quality strategy across several teams or a product line
Design of quality gates integrated into the delivery pipeline
Definition of a quality metrics program that informs release decisions
Facilitation of blameless defect retrospectives and prevention
Coordination of user acceptance testing and beta programs at product scale
Ability to balance testing depth against release timelines with evidence
Establishment of test data and environment management practices
complementaryCompetencies: Understanding of observability signals to catch quality issues in production
Familiarity with compliance testing requirements for the domain
Understanding of when to invest in automation versus manual coverage
indicators: Designs the QA strategy for a complete product autonomously
Leads quality improvement initiatives with measurable outcomes
Defines the quality metrics leadership relies on

coreCompetencies: Definition of the quality strategy across a product portfolio
Establishment of risk-based testing as an organizational practice
Design of release readiness criteria and go/no-go processes
Integration of production quality signals back into test planning
Definition of quality objectives and their supporting metrics
Alignment of quality practices with regulatory and compliance needs
Leadership of quality process improvement across teams
complementaryCompetencies: Understanding of cost and coverage trade-offs across the portfolio
Familiarity with the testing needs of new product areas
Understanding of sustainable, maintainable quality practices
indicators: Shapes quality across multiple products
Leads quality process change across the organization
Defines the long-term quality direction with leadership

coreCompetencies: Leadership of the quality culture across the engineering organization
Definition of quality standards and their governance
Establishment of quality engineering practices beyond pure testing
Use of predictive quality analytics to focus testing effort
Design of defect-prevention programs rather than detection alone
Alignment of the quality function with customer outcomes
Definition of quality practices that scale with the organization
complementaryCompetencies: Contribution to internal quality standards and playbooks
Understanding of testing practices adopted across teams
Understanding of how quality investment maps to business value
indicators: Sets the quality philosophy for the organization
Builds and leads quality communities of practice
Recognized internally as the authority on quality

coreCompetencies: Definition of a multi-year quality strategy for the organization
Positioning of quality as a driver of customer trust and retention
Establishment of a QA center of excellence and its operating model
Definition of quality governance across business units
Evaluation of quality risk in acquisitions and integrations
Justification of quality investment through measurable return
Guidance of tooling and vendor decisions for the quality function
complementaryCompetencies: Definition of quality methodologies reused across the company
Understanding of the testing vendor and tooling landscape
Understanding of quality benchmarking against the industry
indicators: Owns the multi-year quality strategy
Represents the company on quality in external forums
Influences quality direction beyond the organization

coreCompetencies: Executive ownership of quality and its alignment with business goals
Definition of the enterprise quality vision and operating model
Accountability for quality outcomes and the metrics that track them
Leadership of quality organizations across regions and products
Management of the quality function's budget and return
Positioning of quality as a differentiator in the market
Definition of the risk posture that quality practices enforce
complementaryCompetencies: Influence on quality practices across the wider industry
Understanding of quality's role in customer and partner relationships
Understanding of long-term investment in the quality function
indicators: Defines and executes the enterprise quality vision
Accountable for quality outcomes at the company level
Recognized leader in software quality
